_schema.purpose: Registry of Berkeley budget documents. Each entry tracks what the document reveals about spending priorities, structural balance, and the gap between stated policy and actual allocation. Follows the same pattern as audit_findings.json: the document establishes facts; the council's choices against those facts are the scored events.

status_values.pending_review: Document obtained but not yet extracted
status_values.extracted: Key facts captured in source extract file
status_values.scored: Findings incorporated into pipeline scoring or incidents

fields.document_ref: Unique key; matches extract filename in sources/budgets/
fields.title: Official document title
fields.structural_gap: Documented structural deficit or surplus, if stated
fields.key_signals: What this document reveals about council priorities
fields.source_extract: Path to detailed extract file, if created

fy2026_adopted.title: City of Berkeley Adopted Budget FY2026
key_signals: Budget adopted unanimously at 1am — no member voted no or moved to reduce any line item
FTE count and salary/benefit lines reflect the 30% staffing growth documented in the April 2026 financial condition audit
Community development and housing continue as the fastest-growing categories

fy2027_proposed.title: City of Berkeley Proposed Budget FY2027
key_signals: The April 2026 financial condition audit projects a $32M structural deficit for FY2027 — this budget will reveal how the council proposes to address it
Key question: does the proposed budget include any recurring expenditure reductions, or is the gap addressed through one-time measures again?
Council response to the structural deficit in this document is the highest-priority scoring event of 2026
